reviewed_items.reviewer_notes: An attacker can trigger this via P2P gossip by crafting two valid blocks at the same slot (as a malicious proposer) with different bodies, then broadcasting data column sidecars for the same column index from both blocks. The deduplication cache uses blockRootHex (hash of full BeaconBlockHeader including bodyRoot) as the key, allowing both sidecars to be accepted and cached separately, violating the spec requirement that deduplication must be based on tuple (slot, proposer_index, index). Spec reference from outputs/01e_PARTIAL_W2B3_1771748669.json line 281: 'Deduplication key (slot, proposer_index, sidecar.index) must prevent duplicate acceptance.' The code at target_workspace/packages/beacon-node/src/chain/seenCache/seenGossipBlockInput.ts:282-299 confirms that blockRootHex is used as the cache key. This allows equivocating proposers to bypass deduplication and have multiple distinct data columns accepted for the same (slot, proposer_index, column_index) tuple. Impact: enables equivocation attacks in PeerDAS protocol, potentially affecting data availability guarantees. Severity: Medium based on single-client bug affecting >5% of network (Lodestar's market share).
reviewed_items.spec_reference: Deduplication key (slot, proposer_index, sidecar.index) must prevent duplicate acceptance.
